content.problemStatement:
Write a program to find whether a given string is palindrome or not using stack.

Palindrome:

A palindrome is a word, phrase, number, or other sequence of characters that reads the same forward and backward, ignoring spaces, punctuation, and capitalization. In other words, if you reverse the order of characters in a palindrome, it will still match the original sequence.


Input Format
The input consists of a single string.

The string may contain spaces, alphabets, and special characters.



Output Format
Print "Palindrome" if the given string is a palindrome.

Print "Not palindrome" if the given string is not a palindrome.
constraints: Only alphanumeric characters are considered for palindrome checking; case is ignored
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S (0 ≤ |S| ≤ 1000) which may include spaces, letters, digits and special characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print "Palindrome" if the processed string is a palindrome, otherwise print "Not palindrome". Each answer is printed on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The string contains only alphanumeric characters. After converting to lower case it remains "racecar", which reads the same forward and backward.

sampleTestCases.input:
1
A man a plan a canal Panama
sampleTestCases.explanation: Filtering out spaces and non‑alphanumerics gives "amanaplanacanalpanama", a palindrome.
After removing the '@' symbols and ignoring case we obtain "racecar", which is a palindrome.

hidden.label: stress/general mixed cases
hidden.input:
4
Madam
12321
Was it a car or a cat I saw
abc123cba

solution.approach: For each test case we first filter the input string, keeping only alphanumeric characters and converting them to lower case. The filtered sequence is then pushed onto a stack (implemented with a vector/array). By popping from the stack we obtain the characters in reverse order; we compare them one‑by‑one with the original filtered sequence. If any mismatch occurs the string is not a palindrome, otherwise it is. This directly follows the definition of a palindrome and uses the stack as required by the statement.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) per test case where L is the length of the input string (after filtering). Overall O(total characters) for the whole input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) auxiliary space per test case for the filtered array and the stack.
